name: Indus Basin
depletionRate: Very High — world's largest contiguous irrigation system
description: The Indus basin is among the most water-stressed major basins on Earth. Pakistan's Indus irrigation network — the world's largest contiguous system — withdraws over 70% of available flow. Karakoram and Hindu Kush glaciers provide 40% of dry-season flow but face uncertain futures. The 2022 floods (displacing 33 million) paradoxically occurred in a chronically water-scarce basin, illustrating extreme variability.
